fields.summary: Capacity scheduler should support refresh queue automatically by a thread policy.

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description:
In fair scheduler, it is supported that refresh queue related conf automatically by a thread to reload, but in capacity scheduler we only support to refresh queue related changes by refreshQueues, it is needed for our cluster to realize queue manage.

cc [~wangda] [~ztang] [~pbacsko] [~snemeth] [~gandras]  [~bteke] [~shuzirra]

Thank you [~zhuqi] for the patch. Could you elaborate on the necessity of this feature please? Refreshing a queue only makes sense when you change them, and this is already implemented when mutating queue properties via the mutation API. Additionally, I think this is a non-negligible performance hit on the scheduler and I think there should be a well founded reason to do it.

Thank you for the clarification [~zhuqi]. If I understand correctly, you are changing the configuration file itself and calling refresh on CS would be impractical. One way to avoid unnecessary refreshes would be to incorporate the same logic FS uses, namely check, if configuration file is changed since last check, and only call update, when it is needed.
Thanks [~gandras] for your confirm, i will deep into it, find a better way to update it as you suggested.:D

[~gandras]

I have updated a patch for review, update conf if configuration file is changed since last check, and only call update, when it is needed.

Thanks.

 

[~bteke]  [~ztang] [~shuzirra] [~pbacsko] [~snemeth] 

If you any other advice about this?

I think it is a important gap for FS users changed to CS, and i try to fix it to use in our production clusters.

Thanks.

Thank you [~zhuqi] for the patch. Could you elaborate on the necessity of this feature please? Refreshing a queue only makes sense when you change them, and this is already implemented when mutating queue properties via the mutation API. Additionally, I think this is a non-negligible performance hit on the scheduler and I think there should be a well founded reason to do it.

Thank you for the clarification [~zhuqi]. If I understand correctly, you are changing the configuration file itself and calling refresh on CS would be impractical. One way to avoid unnecessary refreshes would be to incorporate the same logic FS uses, namely check, if configuration file is changed since last check, and only call update, when it is needed.
Thanks [~gandras] for your confirm, i will deep into it, find a better way to update it as you suggested.:D

[~gandras]

I have updated a patch for review, update conf if configuration file is changed since last check, and only call update, when it is needed.

Thanks.

 

[~bteke]  [~ztang] [~shuzirra] [~pbacsko] [~snemeth] 

If you any other advice about this?

I think it is a important gap for FS users changed to CS, and i try to fix it to use in our production clusters.

Thanks.
